close_reason: Already fixed: code uses svc.to_string().into() not format\!
description:
secretx-aws-sm/src/lib.rs:107, 112, 137, 142 use 'format!("{svc}").into()' to box a single Display value as Box<dyn Error + Send + Sync>. format! always allocates a new String via the format machinery; '.to_string()' invokes Display directly without the format-args plumbing.

clippy::useless_format under pedantic lints flags this. The fix is mechanical:

    source: format!("{svc}").into(),    →    source: svc.to_string().into(),

Cross-reference: this is the same finding as crate-secretsx-97mw for secretx-aws-kms (lines 244, 250, 296, 301). Both crates should be fixed in the same sweep when one is fixed.

What goes wrong: compiles to slightly more code (extra format machinery and arg slice), and a Rust reader pauses at format! to ask 'what dynamic interpolation needs format_args here?' and finds the answer is 'none'.
title: secretx-aws-sm: format!("{svc}").into() should be svc.to_string().into()
